nodes.name: WrapperPredictionSignalComputation
nodes.description: Entry-point pure wrapper that consumes prediction and raw signal and returns a deterministic result with no persistent state.
nodes.type_signature: (prediction: model prediction input, raw_signal: raw signal input) -> computed wrapper result
nodes.conceptual_summary: Deterministic Dual-Input Functional Combiner. Maps an ordered pair of inputs through a fixed, side-effect-free rule to produce a single derived output. The operation performs a stateless fusion of a precomputed estimate and a raw observation representation, with no internal memory or parameter updates. For identical input values, the mapping is referentially transparent and always returns the same result.. Applications: Robotics: combine a dynamics-model estimate with live sensor arrays to produce a control-ready state signal, Computational biology: merge a learned structural prior with experimental measurement matrices to generate a refined candidate representation, Remote sensing: fuse model-generated environmental fields with raw instrument grids for a corrected analysis layer, Industrial process control: combine predictor outputs and streaming telemetry into a deterministic actuator input metric

nodes.name: NormalizePredictionForPeakScoring
nodes.description: Scale the prediction signal into a normalized score space used by downstream peak detection.

nodes.name: DetectCandidatePeaksWithAdaptiveWindows
nodes.description: Scan normalized scores left-to-right, trigger on threshold crossings, and select one candidate index per window using raw-signal tie-breaking when multiple maxima occur.

outputs.name: candidate_indices
outputs.constraints: indices from thresholded windows; uses long window=15, tail window=7, threshold=0.70

nodes.name: SuppressPeaksCloserThanMinimumDistance
nodes.description: Resolve neighboring candidate peaks that are too close by greedily keeping the one with higher raw-signal amplitude.

outputs.name: distance_filtered_indices
outputs.constraints: adjacent indices differ by at least 35 after suppression

nodes.name: FilterNonPositivePeaksAndFinalize
nodes.description: Remove peaks whose raw-signal amplitude is non-positive and return the remaining indices as a numpy array.

nodes.name: SignalArrayNormalization
nodes.description: Pure stateless normalization of an input numeric array.
nodes.type_signature: (arr: array-like numeric values) -> array-like numeric values
nodes.conceptual_summary: DeterministicNumericArrayRescaling. Transforms a finite numeric array by applying a deterministic scaling rule parameterized by summary statistics of that same array (for example, magnitude, extrema, or dispersion). The mapping preserves the original shape while converting values to a standardized scale so comparisons are less sensitive to absolute magnitude. This is a one-pass, stateless canonicalization step used to make downstream computations scale-consistent.. Applications: Preconditioning multichannel instrument readings in autonomous systems before sensor fusion, Standardizing voxel/intensity arrays in volumetric imaging pipelines prior to segmentation or registration, Normalizing high-throughput molecular measurement vectors before clustering or manifold learning, Rescaling node or edge feature vectors in graph-based infrastructure simulations before optimization
